pattern: Since the whole array is generated by a fixed rule, you never need to materialize it separately from folding it - build and combine each term in the same pass.
hints: You do not need a separate array: the ith term is always start + 2*i, so it can be produced on the fly.
XOR is associative and commutative, so folding the generated terms one at a time in any order gives the same final result.
Start an accumulator at 0, XOR in start + 2*i for i from 0 to n-1, and return the accumulator.

edge_inputs.description: single term returns that term unchanged
start at zero with several terms
maximum n and maximum start together
a run length that is a multiple of four, where a naive parity shortcut would wrongly guess zero

stress.description: the full range of n and start
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    start = rng.randint(0, 1000)
    return [n, start]
